lead_profile_text: First-time property investor trained through Touchstone Education pursuing a rent-to-rent SA strategy. Viewing a 2-bed house in Warwick; owners in France, confirmed SA basis verbally. Previously ruled out R2R deals in London (90-day rule/licensing), Cambridge, and Cardiff (figures didn't stack). Decision expected within the week. Follow-up from entry 039.
emotional_profile: Detail-oriented and analytical — wants the math to work clearly. Fast mover from the Touchstone pipeline. Primary blocker was income achievability, which was resolved once Zac walked through actual local comparable data rather than deal-source estimates.
delay_trigger_phrases: He needs to see the furnishing quote to make a final decision
Figures from deal sources felt unverified
Tenants vacating end of the month
delay_timeline: Decision expected within the week; tenants vacating end of June; furnishing quote pending
green_light_trigger_phrases: Tenants vacating end of the month

top_objections.text: Figures from deal sources felt unverified
top_objections.zac_response: Walked through actual local comparable data showing ADR £219, 68% occupancy, ~£2,100/month average net payout within 1.5km

top_objections.text: Licensing/planning permission concern for third-room conversion
top_objections.zac_response: Confirmed not a material change of use, not HMO; recommended switching from council tax to business rates (~1 year, likely zero rate)

top_objections.text: Whether one bathroom works for three potential bedrooms
top_objections.zac_response: Noted as a practical factor; not fully resolved

top_objections.text: Tight margins — rent + bills ~£1,700/month vs ~£2,099/month average payout
top_objections.zac_response: Full-rate profit ~£399/month; Year 1 ramp essentially break-even acknowledged honestly

top_questions.text: Is third-room conversion (dining room to bedroom) viable?
top_questions.answer: Recommended converting if rooms are large enough; otherwise games room with sofa bed

top_questions.text: Does a third-room conversion require planning permission?
top_questions.answer: Not a material change of use, not HMO — no planning required

what_moved_them: Zac walking through actual local comparable data rather than deal-source/tool-generated estimates
HS2/M4 Junction 15 contractor demand driver — directly relevant to the property location (~12 min drive)
Full clarity on licensing and planning (concern completely resolved)
R2R model viability once comps and demand drivers were clearly explained
what_fell_flat: Initial figures from Vishal's deal sources — felt unverified and undermined confidence before Zac intervened with comp data

conversion_gaps.gap: Furnishing quote needed before final decision
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Send furnishing quote after receiving deal sheet and AI photos from Vishal

conversion_gaps.gap: Year 1 ramp-up is effectively break-even on current rent+bills margin
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Frame third-room conversion as the margin uplift that makes Year 1 viable

zac_income_intro: Comparable 2-bed properties within 1.5km average ~£2,100/month net. Best-performing property in the area achieves ~£46k gross annually. ADR £219, occupancy 68%.
zac_reassurance_phrases: Not a material change of use — no planning required
HS2 contractors are ~12 minutes from the property — that's your core demand driver
Switch from council tax to business rates — likely zero rate within a year
